nameMeanings.name: Johanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Darcy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the English and Irish surname, originally a Norman baronial name (d'Arcy) borne by a family who came from Arcy in northern France. The surname was well established in north central England from the Middle Ages onwards, and various gentry families used it as a male given name from the late 1500s. It has always had a somewhat aristocratic flavour, which has added to its popularity as a first name. It is the surname of the hero of Jane Austen's novel Pride and Prejudice ( 1813 ). Its use as a girl's name, often spelled Darcey —as in the case of the British ballerina Darcey Bussell ( b. 1969 )—is more recent and is now predominant.

en.lifeSummary: When Johanna Darcy was born in 1425, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Darcy, was 11422 and her mother, Joan Greystoke, was 17. She had at least 7 sons and 2 daughters with Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB. She died in Towton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johanna Darcy est née en 1425, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Darcy, avait 11422 ans et sa mère, Joan Greystoke, avait 17 ans. Elle a eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles avec Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB. Elle est décédée à Towton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johanna Darcy 1425 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Darcy 11422 und ihre Mutter Joan Greystoke 17. Sie hatte mit Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb in Towton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Johanna Darcy è nata il nel 1425 nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John Darcy, aveva 11422 anni e sua madre, Joan Greystoke, aveva 17 anni. Lei avuto almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie con Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB. Morì nel Towton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Johanna Darcy(이)가 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Darcy(은)는 11422세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Greystoke(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 Sir John de Beaumont, 1st Viscount, KN, KB(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 타우턴에서 사망했다.
